bays.desc: Drive in, drive out — clean in 4½ minutes. No brushes. No swirl marks. No risk to fresh paint or ceramic coatings. Roosevelt Rd's heated indoor bays let you wash year-round, even at -10°.
Wash it your way. Nine settings — from engine cleaner to Durashield gloss to spot-free rinse — for $4 every 5 minutes. Full dial walkthrough below.

result.heading: Sit back, or **do it yourself**.
result.subhead: Hands-free touchless automatic washes, or self-serve bays with foaming brushes when you want to do it yourself.

result.body: Subscribe for occasional Forest Park car-care tips, token discounts, seasonal promos, and the occasional free wash.

result.a: A touchless automatic wash uses high-pressure water and chemical pre-soaks to lift dirt off your car — no brushes, cloth strips, or rotating tracts ever touch the paint. You drive in, line up on the green light, put it in park, and the system pre-soaks, soaps, rinses, and air-dries your car in about 4 minutes 30 seconds.
result.q: How does a touchless car wash work?

result.a: Pull in and pay at the meter ($4.00 for 5 minutes — cash, card, tap, or Apple Pay), then dial through the nine settings: engine cleaner, tire & wheel cleaner, low-pressure presoak, high-pressure detergent, foaming brush, high-pressure rinse, clear coat sealant, Durashield, and spot-free rinse. Always work top-down, let the presoak dwell 10–20 seconds, and finish on spot-free rinse — never Durashield as the last step.
result.q: How do I use a self-serve car wash bay?

result.a: For regular customers, yes. Each token equals one Ultimate wash (a $10 value). The 10-pack is $85 (save $5) and the 25-pack is $200 (save $25). Tokens never expire, work at both Forest Park locations, and skip the cash station — they make decent gifts too.
result.q: Are car wash tokens worth it?

result.a: Tokens are sold in packs: 5 for $45, 10 for $85 (save $5), and 25 for $200 (save $25). Each token redeems for one Ultimate-tier wash, normally $10.
result.q: What does a wash token cost?

result.a: Both Spotless Carwash locations in Forest Park are open 7am–10pm, every day. The automatic and self-serve bays are card-and-cash operated at the bay — Visa, Mastercard, Amex, tap, Apple Pay, cash, and wash tokens accepted.

The Roosevelt Road location has heated, enclosed automatic bays for winter washing — your car is washed and air-dried inside the bay so doors and locks don't freeze on the way out. The Madison Street location has open-fronted bays.
result.q: Are your bays heated in winter?

result.a: Spotless Carwash has two locations in Forest Park, IL: 7343 Roosevelt Road and 7802 Madison Street. Both sites have automatic touchless bays and self-serve wand bays, and both are open 7am–10pm daily.
result.q: Where are you located in Forest Park?

result.a: No. Spotless wash tokens never expire and are not site-restricted — buy them once, use them whenever, at either Forest Park location.

result.lede: Our automatic bays run on a simple traffic-light system. Wait for green, pull forward slow, hit the stop signal, and let the timer count down.

steps.description: Tap, card, cash, or token — Apple Pay too. Pick your wash (8, 9, 10, or 12) and confirm.
steps.title: Pay at the station

steps.description: Don't pull in unless you have a green light. Red & yellow mean wait.
steps.title: Wait for green

steps.description: Pull forward slowly. Hit the RED STOP signal, then put your car in park and sit tight.
steps.title: Pull in & park

steps.description: Check the countdown timer straight ahead. When the door opens and the dryers finish, pull through.
steps.title: Watch the timer

result.tip:
Ten premium wash settings at your fingertips — tire & wheel cleaner, low-pressure presoak, high-pressure detergent, foaming brush, high-pressure rinse, clear coat sealant, Durashield surface gloss, spot-free rinse and air shammee dryer.

$4.00 for 5 mins.

For best results, work top-to-bottom. Let presoak dwell 10–20 seconds before rinsing, and always finish with the spot-free rinse — never Durashield as the last step.

quotes.text: Pulled in right before close after a Chicago snowstorm. Heated bay, four and a half minutes, drove out spotless. The only place that does it right.
Self-serve bays have everything you need. The Durashield + spot-free combo is the move. Tokens make the price feel like nothing.
My salt-covered SUV looked brand new in under five minutes. Touchless and zero scratches — I will not go anywhere else.
